Explains a service and starts it ‚Äî one prominent **Start now** action;
sets expectations (documents, fees in Birr, timing) up front so the
citizen isn't surprised mid-flow.

```tsx
import { ServiceLandingPage } from '@ethds/templates';

<ServiceLandingPage
  serviceName="Immigration and Citizenship Service"
  languages={languages}
  locale="en"
  onLocale={(code) => { /* ‚Ä¶ */ }}
  breadcrumb={breadcrumb}
  heading="Apply for a passport"
  summary="Who this service is for and what it does, in plain language."
  onStart={() => { /* navigate to the Service Application Page */ }}
  requirements={['Your Fayda (national ID) number', 'A recent photo', 'Payment of ETB 350.00']}
/>
```

## When to use this template

Use this before any [Service Application Page](service-application-page) ‚Äî
routing **Start now** to it is the caller's responsibility (via
`onStart`).

## Accessibility & localization

- One `<h1>` (the service name); the breadcrumb shows where the citizen
  is in the hierarchy.
- Requirements render as a real list, not a wall of prose, so a screen
  reader announces the count and each item.
- `heading`, `summary`, and every requirement are translated content
  passed in by the caller.

## Props

| Prop | Type | Required |
|---|---|---|
| `serviceName` | `ReactNode` | Yes |
| `nav` | `HeaderNavItem[]` | No |
| `languages` | `LanguageOption[]` | Yes |
| `locale` | `string` | Yes |
| `onLocale` | `(code: string) => void` | Yes |
| `breadcrumb` | `BreadcrumbItem[]` | Yes |
| `heading` | `ReactNode` | Yes |
| `summary` | `ReactNode` | Yes |
| `onStart` | `() => void` | Yes |
| `requirements` | `ReactNode[]` | Yes |
| `footerGroups` | `FooterLinkGroup[]` | No |
| `copyright` | `ReactNode` | No |
| `labels` | `Partial<ServiceLandingPageLabels>` | No |
